methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
GHGSat is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Stephane Germain that has raised $67M+. The valuation analysis draws on a broader universe of 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, though narrow peer matching proved constrained.

Only one valuation method was applicable given data constraints. Stage-Progression analysis, using the $67M total raised as a proxy anchor and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to subsequent stages, implies a $25M valuation. This yields a triangulated range of $21M to $29M, with $25M as the median point. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied because GHGSat's revenue is not disclosed, a common limitation with private companies; the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 25.8x revenue across the 6 public peers identified. Peer-Median methodology was also inapplicable due to insufficient datapoints in the narrow same-stage and adjacent-stage filters within the sector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M–$29M, centered at $25M. Key uncertainties stem from the single-method reliance and the absence of revenue or profitability metrics. A deeper valuation would benefit from GHGSat's actual financial performance, capital efficiency relative to peers, and progress toward subsequent funding rounds.
